hero.body: decreos turns a group chat into a constitutional council republic - citizens, council offices, votes, invitations, and an official record that outlives the feed.

method.body: Less explanation. More gravity. Four reasons a friend group earns the weight of an institution.
method.featureBody: The decision gets a place to stand.

articles.title: A place
articles.body: A room with a name has a different gravity. Convene where it counts.

articles.title: An official record
articles.body: Not a vibe check. An entry the group can return to and cite.

articles.title: A memory
articles.body: Council offices, votes, and constitution changes stay visible enough to matter.

articles.title: A ritual
articles.body: Friends stay unserious without making every choice disposable.

record.title: Social media forgets. The record does not.
record.body: Motions, votes, offices, invitations, admissions, court remedies, and constitution changes are entered, numbered, and kept. Some things should survive the feed.

social.body: Comments, likes, and reactions can surround a motion without becoming constitutional mechanics. The social layer carries the argument; votes, court remedies, and official records preserve the constitutional trail.

motion.label: Motion No. 027
motion.author: introduced by @mira
motion.text: Adopt Taco Tuesday as a standing resolution.

social.caption: Reactions and replies shape the room; votes decide and official records preserve the republic

name.label: Name your polity
examples: The Council for Very Minor Decisions
The Committee to Decide Where We Eat

preset.title: Use the council republic preset.
preset.description: A single-polity constitutional republic with a citizens' council, elected offices, and court remedies.
preset.label: Preset

standardConstitutionalCouncilRepublic.label: Standard Constitutional Council Republic
standardConstitutionalCouncilRepublic.copy: Citizens, councilors, steward, tribune, magistrates' court, official records, and typed constitution changes.
standardConstitutionalCouncilRepublic.description: The recommended setup keeps authority bounded through council proceedings, court remedies, elected offices, and a short bootstrap steward.

pace.title: Set the pace.
pace.description: Choose how much notice ordinary votes should have.
pace.label: Pace

fast.label: Fast
fast.description: A 7-day bootstrap and one-day ordinary votes for groups that meet often.

standard.label: Standard
standard.description: A 14-day bootstrap with two-day ordinary votes that move quickly without surprising citizens.

deliberate.label: Deliberate
deliberate.description: A 30-day bootstrap and four-day ordinary votes for groups that need slower proceedings.

visibility.title: Choose the door.
visibility.description: Public appears in discovery. Private stays out of discovery.
visibility.label: Access

public.label: Public
public.description: Lets people find and read what is happening.

invites.title: Invite the first people.
invites.description: One or two invitations is enough to begin.
invites.label: Person to invite

ready.title: {{name}} is ready.
ready.description: The founding room is prepared. Enter and convene.

errors.emailDuplicate: That person is already on the list.
